evals.prompt: You just finished a task that fixed a bug in src/sync.py (with a test) and separately bumped the coverage ratchet in pyproject.toml. Finish up.
evals.expected_output: The agent makes two commits on main — one per concern, imperative subjects under 72 chars — pushes after each, and ends with the checklist clean: on main, git status clean, nothing unpushed. No single 'updates' blob commit, no branch, no PR.

evals.prompt: git push was rejected: 'Updates were rejected because the remote contains work that you do not have locally.'
evals.expected_output: The agent runs git pull --rebase and pushes again. It does not force-push main, does not create a branch, and surfaces any rebase conflict rather than resolving destructively.

evals.prompt: Commit this cleanup. By the way, the repo seems to be on a branch called experiment-old.
evals.expected_output: The agent stops and reports the repo is on experiment-old before doing anything — it does not silently merge, switch, or commit to the branch — and asks how Jacob wants to reconcile it with main.
